pages.notes: Index page mapping diary page numbers to places/trips. Left column numbers are partially cut off at the left margin — first digits unclear for entries in the lower teens/twenties range. Entry 24 appears in both columns (continuation). Right column contains page references (42, 45?, 46, 47?) at far right edge. 'Rosson Wye' likely 'Ross on Wye'. 'Eardistand' could be 'Eardisland'. 'Groft Castle' could be 'Croft Castle'. 'Rhaydet' likely 'Rhayader'. 'Wheadstead' could be 'Wheathampstead'. 'Barpols' could be 'Bargols' or 'Barpols'. 'Cotignant' may be 'Cotignac'. 'Rayd' possibly 'Raoud' or similar. 'Laires' possibly 'Laives'. 'Burey' possibly 'Buxy'. 'Northe[p] Curnow' possibly 'Northop' and 'Curnow' (a person's name?).

pages.notes: Continuation of index page from left half. Entry 44 includes year '2021' — this is notable as it is later than the stated volume range of 2003-2019, suggesting the index was updated after the main diary period or the year annotation is a page reference rather than a date. Entry 40 appears missing (jumps from 39 to 41). 'Nettorston' could be 'Nanthorton' or 'Nettleton'. 'Cha[d]worth Roman Villa' may be 'Chedworth Roman Villa'. 'Dolwyddelan' is a castle in North Wales. 'hlan Bleanow Festiniog' likely 'Blaenau Ffestiniog'. 'Llandanwg' is a village near Harlech. 'St. Tanwg' is correct (patron saint of Llandanwg). 'Hellgir Pass' may be 'Hellgill' or 'Bwlch y Groes'.
